marketState: Neutral, tilted bearish near-term - storage surplus and a broad technical downtrend offset by price already sitting well below its seasonal median
summary: Front-month NG at $2.897 is essentially at fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417), so there is no valuation edge right now, and the 7/14/21-day fair-value projections needed to gauge the trajectory are unavailable. Price is running about 11.6 percentage points below its seasonal median (-15.0% YTD vs -3.4% median), a long-run bullish anomaly, yet the 16-year seasonality history actually skews bearish over the next 1-4 weeks (only 31% odds of being higher in 4 weeks, typical move -3.1%) and carries Low reliability (50pp spread). Weather demand is running about +4% above the 5-year normal but slipped -8 over the last 24h, and the granular near-term (1-7D) and medium-term (8-16D) demand reads needed to time entries are both missing from this snapshot. Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average after a large +61 Bcf weekly build - a bearish fundamental overhang - while technicals confirm a broad downtrend across 4h-1w (bearish EMA structure, negative MACD), though daily RSI near 34 and price below the lower daily Bollinger band suggest the move may already be stretched. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range (room either way), and the futures curve is unavailable, so contango/backwardation can't be assessed.

longTerm.rationale: Price is unusually cheap vs its seasonal median (-11.6pp), a bullish mean-reversion argument heading toward seasonally-strong September, but the 4-week seasonality odds actually skew down (31% higher, -3.1% typical) with Low reliability, and the +184 Bcf storage surplus is a genuine bearish fundamental headwind. Missing weather demand_1_7D/8_16D and forward fair-value bands (plus7D/14D/21D) remove the two inputs that would normally confirm timing, so the cheap-vs-median case is not yet actionable.
longTerm.invalidation: If the storage surplus keeps widening and price breaks convincingly below the fair-value band floor (~$2.42) without a seasonal bounce into August/September, the cheap-vs-median thesis fails and further downside becomes the base case.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for weather demand_1_7D/8_16D and forward fair-value data to populate, or for a pullback toward the $2.60-$2.75 zone (seasonal-cheap entry ahead of Sep seasonal strength) with storage surprises turning supportive, before sizing a long.

shortTerm.rationale: Technicals are broadly bearish from 4h through 1w (death-cross EMAs, negative MACD, 1d close below the lower Bollinger band), aligning with the storage surplus, but daily/4h RSI near 32-37 signals the down-move is already stretched and price sits right at fair value with no edge either way. Near-term weather (demand_1_7D) and the futures curve - two of the four short-term priority inputs - are both missing, so there isn't enough to time an entry with confidence.
shortTerm.invalidation: A clean reclaim of the 1d/4h EMA structure (50 crossing back above 200) with RSI recovering above 50 would flip the near-term bias from bearish to neutral/bullish.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for near-term weather demand and futures-curve data to populate, or for a decisive break below ~$2.75 (confirming bearish continuation) to short, or a bounce into $3.05-$3.15 (fair-value/EMA resistance) to fade.

keyDrivers: Price ~ fair value ($2.897 vs $2.917 fair, band $2.417-$3.417) - no valuation edge currently
Storage +184 Bcf vs 5y with a +61 Bcf weekly build - bearish fundamental overhang
Technicals broadly bearish 4h-1w (EMA death cross, negative MACD) but daily RSI ~34 near oversold
Seasonality: price -11.6pp below median (cheap) yet 4-week outlook skews down (-3.1% typical, 31% odds higher)
Weather demand +4% vs 5y normal but cooling -8 over 24h; granular near/medium-term reads missing
risks: Weather demand_1_7D/8_16D and futures-curve data are missing, so near-term catalysts can't be directly observed
Seasonality reliability is Low (50pp 10-90% spread) - wide dispersion of historical outcomes
Managed-money net-short positioning is only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, leaving room for a sharp short-covering rally on any bullish surprise (storage draw, heat event)
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
